evidence_summary: his alleged would-be destroyer, and not that of someone else who may have been “pointed”? at in mistake. He therefore, in company with the doctor who procured it, will in a few days’ time, to avert suspicion, leave the camp after dark, and in some sequest ered spot kindle a fire, over which they warm the bone-apparatus with its ghostly contents. Leaving the medicine-man to keep watch, he returns to camp, divulges his mission to no one, but enters into con- versation with his alleged assailant on any ordinary everyday topic, watches most carefully to see if he is at all sick or not, and may casually get him to admit that he is.
